The TER compares the power difference between the reported power consumption by the O-RU

NETCONF Server and the measured power consumption in step 2 and validates it is within acceptable

range, e.g. ± 10%.

4.

The TER NETCONF Client sends deep-hibernate rpc command.

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="105">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<deep-hibernate>

<hibernate-time>15</hibernate-time>

</deep-hibernate>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

NOTE: hibernate-time is between min-hibernate-time-duration and max-hibernate-time-duration if

advertised by the O-RU.

5.

The O-RU NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> message indicating successful completion of

requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="105">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

6.

The TER NETCONF Client waits for deep-hibernate-activated notification.

For Example,

<notification xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<deep-hibernate-activated/>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

107

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<hibernate-time>15</hibernate-time>

<deep-hibernate-activated/>

</notification>

7.

The O-RU NETCONF Server starts deep-hibernate timer.

8.

The TER NETCONF Client sends close-session rpc command.

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="106">

<close-session xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0"/>

</rpc>

9.

The O-RU NETCONF Server enters deep-hibernate.

10. Half of hibernate time past, the TER performs the DC or AC power consumption measurement with the

power meter. The power consumption should now be smaller than the previous one measured in the step

2.

11. The TER verifies the transmission from the O-RU is turned OFF using a signal analyser.

12. The O-RU NETCONF Server restarts after the expiry of hibernate-time.

13. After restart, the TER verifies restart-cause value is DEEP-HIBERNATE-RESTART.

14. After carrier activation, the TER NETCONF Client waits for at least two continuous Notifications of

ACTIVE from the O-RU NETCONF Server to ensure a full cycle of ACTIVE state for power measurements

is completed.

15. The TER performs the DC or AC power consumption measurement with the power meter. The power

consumption should now be larger than the previous one measured in the step 10.

16. The TER verifies transmission is present from the O-RU and the expected signal (defined in C.a.4) is

measured successfully with a signal analyser.

 

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

1.

Test steps above are performed successfully.

2.

All validation steps mentioned in step 10 and step 15 are within expected power ranges as per the vendor’s O-

RU product specifications.

3.1.14.6

Data layer control

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-RU. The test shall be executed if the O-RU

supports Data layer control.

This test validates Data layer control.

The test scenario is intentionally limited in scope to be applicable to any O-RU hardware design that

supports Energy Savings.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:

20.3.3 Data layer control

10.2 Measurement activation and de-activation

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TER described in clause 2.1 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-RU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

108

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

1.

The test procedure described in clause 3.1.10.1 is successfully completed so that O-RU is configured to

receive, process and output the test signal defined in initial condition 4.

2.

The O-RU NETCONF Server exposes the capability to support energy savings including Data layer

control.

3.

The TER NETCONF Client subscribes to notifications of the measurement-object power, with the

measurement-interval of 60 sec.

4.

CUSM Emulator generates proper C/U plane stimulus for 3GPP test waveform with 30 kHz subcarrier

spacing and 100 MHz Bandwidth as described in clause ETSI TS 138.141 -1 NR-FR1-TM1.1 and clause

3.2.1.1.5 of this document. If the radio does not support the bandwidth and/or numerology defined here, it

will be up to the test developer to modify these parameters in the test waveform to adapt to the O-RU

capabilities.

a.

Procedure

1.

The TER NETCONF Client retrieves subscribed notifications from the O-RU NETCONF Server on the

power consumption performance measurement.

2.

The TER measures the AC or DC power consumption of the O-RU using power meter.

3.

The TER compares the power difference between the reported power consumption by the O-RU

NETCONF Server and the measured power consumption in step 2 and validates it is within acceptable

range, e.g. ± 10%.

4.

The TER NETCONF Client turns off data layers by adding energy-sharing-group-id into energy-sharing-

groups-disabled leaf-list parameter.

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="103">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<energy-sharing-groups-disabled>4</energy-sharing-groups-

disabled>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

5.

The O-RU NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> message indicating successful completion of

requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="103">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

6.

The TER NETCONF Client waits for 10 seconds.

7.

The TER performs the DC or AC power consumption measurement with the power meter.

8.

The TER compares the difference between the reported power consumption by the O-RU NETCONF

Server and the measured in step 7 to validate it is within acceptable range, e.g. ± 10%. The power

consumption should be smaller than the previous one measured in the step 2.

9.

The TER NETCONF Client turns on data layers by removing energy-sharing-group-id from energy-

sharing-groups-disabled leaf-list parameter after 15 minutes.

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="104">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

109

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

</target>

<config>

<energy-sharing-groups-disabled:operation="delete">4</energy-

sharing-groups-disabled>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

10. The O-RU NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> message indicating successful completion of

requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="104">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

11. The TER NETCONF Client waits for data-layer-control-wakeup-notification.

Example,

<notification xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<data-layer-control-wakeup-notification>

<energy-sharing-group>4</energy-sharing-group>

</data-layer-control-wakeup-notification>

</notification>

12. The TER performs the DC or AC power consumption measurement with the power meter.

13. The TER verifies the expected signal (defined in C.a.4) is measured successfully with a signal analyser. 

14. The TER compares the DC or AC power difference between the reported power consumption by the O-

RU NETCONF Server and the measured DC or AC power consumption in step 12 to validate it is within

acceptable range, e.g. ± 10%. The power consumption should now be larger than the previous one

measured in the step 7.

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

1.

Test steps above are performed successfully.

2.

All validation steps mentioned in step 8 and step 14 are within expected power ranges as per the vendor’s O-

RU product specifications.

3.1a

M-Plane Measurements for O-DU

The O-RAN Fronthaul management plane (M-Plane) protocol conformance tests for O-DU as device under test are

described in this clause. Table 1.2.4-1a lists all M-Plane test cases for O-DU.

3.1a.1

Transport and Handshake Test Scenarios

3.1a.1.1

Transport and Handshake in IPv4/SSH Environment

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This test is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for an O-DU that supports IPV4.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

110

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

This scenario validates that the O-DU properly executes the session establishment procedure with TED NETCONF

Server.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



6.2 Management Plane Transport aspects



6.3 NETCONF Call Home to O-RU Controller(s)

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The O-DU is powered on.

2.

Default SSH credentials (sudo) are used by both the O-DU Netconf Client and the TED NETCONF

Server.

b.

Procedure

1.

Optional step: The TED NETCONF Server performs the DHCPv4 procedure on the preconfigured VLAN

or first VLAN where the Offer from DHCP Server / Relay is received if the scan method is used.

2.

The TED NETCONF Server establishes TCP connection and performs a Call Home procedure towards

the O-DU NETCONF Client.

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client establishes a SSH session with the TED NETCONF Server. Proper

credentials are used.

4.

The TED NETCONF Server accepts the SSH session establishment from the O-DU NETCONF Client

5.

The O-DU NETCONF Client and TED NETCONF Server exchange capabilities through NETCONF

<hello> messages. The following is an abbreviated example of the capabilities exchange. A typical

capabilities exchange will have far more parameters included.

NOTE: RFC 6241 is followed for NETCONF communication.

NETCONF Server (TED) Hello message .

<hello xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<capabilities>

<capability>urn:ietf:params:netconf:base:1.0</capability>

<capability>urn:ietf:params:netconf:base:1.1</capability>

<capability>urn:ietf:params:netconf:capability:writable-

running:1.0</capability>

<capability>urn:ietf:params:netconf:capability:notification:1.0</capabi

lity>

<capability>urn:ietf:params:netconf:capability:interleave:1.0</capabili

ty>

<capability>http://org/openroadm/user-mgmt?module=org-openroadm-

user-mgmt&amp;amp;amp;revision=2017-12-15</capability>

……

</capabilities>

<session-id>1</session-id>

</hello>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

111

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

NETCONF Client (DU) Hello message …

<hello xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<capabilities>

<capability>urn:ietf:params:netconf:base:1.0</capability>

<capability>urn:ietf:params:netconf:base:1.1</capability>

</capabilities>

</hello>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test scenario step 5 is performed successfully.

3.1a.1.2

Transport and Handshake in IPv6/SSH Environment

The test description, applicability, entrance criteria, methodology and requirements are the same as in clause 3.1a.1.1,

Transport and Handshake in IPv4/SSH Environment (positive case). The only difference is that this test is applicable to

IPv6 environments. This test is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU that supports IPV6.

3.1a.1.3

Transport and Handshake in IPv4/TLS Environment

The test description, applicability, entrance criteria, methodology and requirements are the same as in clause 3.1a.1.1,

Transport and Handshake in IPv4/SSH Environment (positive case). The only difference is that this test is applicable to

IPv4 and TLS environments. This test is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU that supports IPV4.

3.1a.1.4

Transport and Handshake in IPv6/TLS Environment

The test description, applicability, entrance criteria, methodology and requirements are the same as in clause 3.1a.1.1

Transport and Handshake in IPv4/SSH Environment (positive case). The only difference is that this test is applicable to

IPv6 and TLS environments. This test is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU that supports IPV6.

3.1a.2

Subscription to Notifications

3.1a.2.1

Subscription to NETCONF Event Stream

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This test is MANDATORY for O-DU.

This test validates that the O-DU properly subscribes the default “NETCONF” event stream for all notifications,

including alarm notifications.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



9.1.7 Notification framework

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

112

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The tests for NETCONF call home, NETCONF connection establishment, and NETCONF capability

discovery are successfully completed. The TED NETCONF Server has indicated its support for

“Notifications” via the NETCONF Hello message.

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers the subscription to the TED NETCONF Server to subscribe to the

default “NETCONF” event stream for all notifications that the TED NETCONF Server can generate

(including alarm notifications), using either of the following two forms of create-subscription RPC:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="0">

<create-subscription

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<stream>NETCONF</stream>

</create-subscription>

</rpc>

or

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="0">

<create-subscription

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

</create-subscription>

</rpc>

NOTE 1: message-id = “0” in only an example in the above RPC

NOTE 2: According to RFC 5277, <stream> is an optional parameter of create-subscription. If it is not

present, events in the default NETCONF stream will be sent.

2.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="0">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

to the O-DU NETCONF Client.

NOTE: RFC 6241 is followed for NETCONF communication.

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

DUT passes this test, if step 1 is performed after all initial conditions are met.

NOTE: There is no specified timeframe within which the DUT is required to perform step 1 following the

fulfilment of all initial conditions.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

113

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

3.1a.3

M-Plane Connection Supervision

3.1a.3.1

M-Plane connection supervision

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for an O-DU with appropriate group privileges namely “sudo”,

“hybrid-odu”, or “carrier”.

This test validates that the O-DU handles the connection supervision process correctly. This scenario corresponds

to clause 6.7, “Monitoring NETCONF connectivity”, in [3].

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 are operational, configured, and connected to the O-

DU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The call home procedure and the test case notification subscription are successfully completed.

b.

Procedure

1. The TED NETCONF Server sends a supervision notification message to the O-DU NETCONF Client.

2.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends to the TED NETCONF Server with

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="20">

<supervision-watchdog-reset xmlns="urn:o-ran:supervision:1.0">

<supervision-notification-interval>60</supervision-

notification-interval>

<guard-timer-overhead>10</guard-timer-overhead>

</supervision-watchdog-reset>

</rpc>

NOTE 1: The values of parameters are examples of message-id, supervision-notification-interval and

guard-timer-overhead.

NOTE 2: O-DU sends supervision-watchdog-reset rpc before supervision-notification-interval+guard-

timer expiry.

3.

The TED NETCONF Server sends a reply to the O-DU NETCONF Client.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="20">

<next-update-at xmlns="urn:o-ran:supervision:1.0">YYYY-MM-

DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ </next-update-at>

</rpc-reply>

NOTE: The values of parameters are examples of message-id and next-update-at.

4.

Steps 1 to 3 are looped for a total of 30 iterations including the first pass through steps 1 to 3.

NOTE: RFC 6241 is followed for NETCONF communication.

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

114

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

Test scenario step 4 is performed successfully in all iterations.

3.1a.4

Retrieval of Netconf Server’s Information Elements

3.1a.4.1

Retrieval of Netconf Server’s Information Elements

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is MANDATORY for O-DU.

This scenario validates that the O-DU properly executes a get command, O-DU can pass this test by executing

either of the two cases: ‘get without filter’ or ‘get with filter’.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:

·

6.6 NETCONF capability discovery

·

9.1.2 Retrieve state

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The tests for NETCONF call home, notification subscription and supervision connection are successfully

completed.

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers either <rpc><get> or <rpc><get><filter> towards the TED

NETCONF Server.

Case i) Example for get-rpc without filter:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="10">

<get xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

</get>

</rpc>

Case ii) Examples for get-rpc with subtree and xpath filters:

NOTE: The O-DU NETCONF Client uses the get RPC together with sub-tree based <filter> and XPATH

based <filter> to recover particular sub-trees from the TED.

rpc that filters all low-level-rx-endpoints using subtree filter:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="3">

<get xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<filter type = ”subtree”>

<user-plane-configuration xmlns="urn:o-ran:uplane-

conf:1.0”>

<low-level-rx-endpoints>

</low-level-rx-endpoints>

</user-plane-configuration>

</filter>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

115

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

</get>

</rpc>

With xpath filter:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="3">

<get xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<filter xmlns:yanglib="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:yang:ietf-yang-

library"

type="xpath"

select="/yanglib:*//."/>

</get>

</rpc>

2.

Case i) The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply><data> where <data> contains all

information elements that the TED NETCONF Server is able to expose.

Case ii) The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply><data> where <data> contains details for

objects as conforming to the <filter>

NOTE: O-DU NETCONF Client should not crash for empty data tag from TED NETCONF Server.

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test step 1-2 is performed successfully.

3.1a.5

Fault Management

This test case is not addressed in the current document.

3.1a.6

Software Update

3.1a.6.1

Software Update and Install

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU with appropriate group privileges namely, “sudo",

“nms” or “swm”.

This test validates that the O-DU can successfully perform a software download and software install procedure.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



8. Software Management

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clauses 3.1a.2.1 and 3.1a.4.1 is successfully completed.

2.

A correct and internally consistent SW package is available for test purposes.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

116

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

3.

The TED NETCONF Server provides at least one slot available for SW update procedure. The status of

this slot is {active = false, running = false, access = READ-WRITE}

4.

A sFTP/FTPES server is available with an account for the SW update procedure on the O-DU, using

FTPES if the NETCONF Server controller is using NETCONF/TLS to configure the TED.

5.

TED NETCONF server has not exposed build-content-download.

6.

Based on the O-DU implementation, software update and installation may be tested either during the

“start-up” procedure (e.g., the TED is not running the desired SW package of the DUT) or alternatively

initiated via a manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED).

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers <rpc><software-download>. The TED NETCONF Server responds

with <rpc-reply><status> as STARTED.

An example rpc for software-download:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="13">

<software-download xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">

<remote-file-path>sftp://oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/software.zip</remote-

file-path>

</software-download>

</rpc>

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="13">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">STARTED</status>

</rpc-reply>

2.

The TED NETCONF Server performs a software download procedure. When the download is completed,

the TED NETCONF Server sends <notification><download-event> with status COMPLETED to O-DU

NETCONF Client

<notification xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime> YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ </eventTime>

<download-event xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">

<file-name>sftp://oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/software.zip</file-name>

<status>COMPLETED</status>

</download-event>

</notification>

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers <rpc><software-install> informing the TED NETCONF Server

about the desired slot for installation. This slot must have the attributes active = FALSE, running =

FALSE, access = READ-WRITE. The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply> with status

STARTED.

An example rpc for software-install:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="14">

<software-install xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">

<slot-name>"SLOT0"</slot-name>

<file-names>"testpkg"</file-names>

</software-install>

</rpc>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

117

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="14">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">STARTED</status>

</rpc-reply>

4.

The TED NETCONF Server sends <notification><install-event><status> to the O-DU NETCONF Client.

Field <status> contains the value COMPLETED to indicate the successful installation of software to the

desired slot.

<notification xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime> YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ </eventTime>

<install-event xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">

<slot-name>SLOT0</slot-name>

<status>COMPLETED</status>

</install-event>

</notification>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

1.

Test steps 1-5 completed successfully, where DU sends software install after software download.

3.1a.7

Software Activation

3.1a.7.1

Software Activation

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU with appropriate group privileges namely, “sudo",

“nms” or “swm”.

This test validates that the O-DU can successfully perform activate software.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



8. Software Management

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clause 3.1a.6.1 is successfully completed.

2.

Based on the O-DU implementation, software activation may be tested either during the “start-up”

procedure or alternatively initiated via a manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from

the TED).

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers <rpc><software-activate> towards the TED NETCONF Server. The

name of a slot containing a valid software installation to be activated is provided as a parameter in the

RPC.

An example rpc for software-activate

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

118

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="15">

<software-activate xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">

<slot-name>SLOT0</slot-name>

</software-activate>

</rpc>

2. The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply><status> as STARTED.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="15">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">STARTED</status>

</rpc-reply>

3.

The TED NETCONF Server sends <notification><activation-event> with a status COMPLETED and the

slot-name in the activation event corresponds to the slot-name used in the software-activate RPC to the O-

DU NETCONF Client.

<notification xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime> YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ </eventTime>

<activation-event xmlns="urn:o-ran:software-management:1.0">

<slot-name>"SLOT0"</slot-name>

<status>COMPLETED</status>

</activation-event>

</notification>

4.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <rpc><reset></rpc> to the TED NETCONF Server.

<reset xmlns=”urn:o-ran:operations:1.0”> </reset>

5.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with rpc-reply.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="2"><ok/></rpc-reply>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

1.

Steps 1-5 are completed successfully

3.1a.8

Access Control

3.1a.8.1

Sudo on M-plane Architecture Models

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for an O-DU supporting the Hybrid M-plane architecture model

with “sudo” or “hybrid-odu” group privileges, or Hierarchical M-plane architecture model with “sudo” privileges.

This test validates that O-DU can correctly configure users applying the user management model.

This scenario corresponds to the following clauses in [3]:

·

6.4 NETCONF Connection Establishment

·

6.5 NETCONF Access Control

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

119

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clause 3.1a.1.1 (IPv4/SSH) or 3.1a.1.3 (IPv6/SSH) or 3.1a.1.5 (IPv4/TLS)

or 3.1a.1.7 (IPv6/TLS) is successfully completed.

2.

Based on the O-DU implementation, new user account creation may be tested either during “start-up”

procedure or alternatively initiated via a manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from

the TED) to configure new user accounts.

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client establishes a connection with the TED NETCONF Server.

2.

The O-DU NETCONF Client configures sudo user account and password using o-ran-user.mgmt.yang.

The ‘nms’, ‘fm-pm’, ‘swm’, ‘carrier’ and ‘hybrid-odu’ privilege groups can optionally be configured.

NOTE 1: For new user accounts which are <account-type>CERTIFICATE</account-type>, <password>

leaf shall not be present.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="16">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<default-operation>merge</default-operation>

<config>

<users xmlns="urn:o-ran:user-mgmt:1.0">

<user>

<name>MPLANEUSER0</name>

<account-type>PASSWORD</account-type>

<password>12345678</password>

<enabled>true</enabled>

</user>

</users>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="17">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client configures user account to group mappings for the one or two new accounts

using ietf-netconf-acm.yang with sudo privilege. The ‘nms’, fm-pm’, swm’, ‘carrier’ and ‘hybrid-odu’

privilege groups can be mapped if created in step 2.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="18">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

120

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

</target>

<default-operation>merge</default-operation>

<config>

<nacm xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:yang:ietf-netconf-acm">

<groups>

<group>

<name>sudo</name>

<user-name>MPLANEUSER0</user-name>

</group>

</groups>

</nacm>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

4. The TED NETCONF Server confirms the operations for the above transactions with below rpc-reply

<rpc-reply message-id="19"

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Step 1 to 4 is correctly validated.

3.1a.9

Netconf Server Configurability

3.1a.9.1

Netconf Server Configurability Test

A. Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU with either "sudo" or "hybrid-odu" access privileges.

This test validates that O-DU configures NETCONF Server as per its module-capabilities and user-plane-

configuration.

This scenario corresponds to the following clauses in [3]:

• 9 Configuration Management

• 15.2 User plane message routing

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane is operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

C. Test Methodology

a.

Initial Condition

1.

The test procedure described in clause 3.1a.4.1 is successfully completed, including retrieval of

module-capabilities and uplane-configuration of TED Netconf Server.

2.

This test scenario is validated as part of Start-up procedure.

3.

The O-DU is configured to setup a cell in the TED.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

121

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

4.

The TED acts as radio, hence DU creates processing-elements, tx/rx-array-carriers, low-level-tx/rx-

endpoints, low-level-tx/rx-links as per module-capabilities and user-plane-configuration of TED.

b.

Procedure

The RPCs in below steps are examples and are expected to vary for different equipment or configurations. The

number of steps in the procedure might vary depending on the DU implementation of edit-config.

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers <rpc><edit-config> to create VLAN Interface for CU-Plane.

rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="1">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<default-operation>merge</default-operation>

<config>

<interfaces xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:yang:ietf-interfaces">

<interface>

<name>cuplane0</name>

<type xmlns:ianaift="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:yang:iana-if-

type">ianaift:l2vlan</type>

<mac-address xmlns="urn:o-

ran:interfaces:1.0">A0:CE:C8:17:7A:41</mac-address>

<vlan-id xmlns="urn:o-ran:interfaces:1.0">1</vlan-id>

</interface>

</interfaces>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

2.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> ok and Netconf config change notification.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="2">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

<notification xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime> YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ </eventTime>

<netconf-config-change xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:yang:ietf-

netconf-notifications">

<changed-by>

<session-id>2</session-id>

<username>oranuser</username>

</changed-by>

<datastore>running</datastore>

<edit>

<target xmlns:o-ran-uplane-conf="urn:o-ran:uplane-conf:1.0">/o-

ran-uplane-conf:user-plane-configuration</target>

<operation>merge</operation>

</edit>

</netconf-config-change>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

122

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

</notification>

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers <rpc><edit-config> to create processing-elements.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="4">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<default-operation>merge</default-operation>

<config>

<processing-elements xmlns="urn:o-ran:processing-element:1.0">

<transport-session-type>ETH-INTERFACE</transport-session-

type>

<ru-elements>

<name>processing-element01</name>

<transport-flow>

<interface-name>cuplane0</interface-name>

<eth-flow>

<ru-mac-address> A0:CE:C8:17:7A:41</ru-mac-address>

<vlan-id>1</vlan-id>

<o-du-mac-address>0a:00:27:00:00:0d</o-du-mac-

address>

</eth-flow>

</transport-flow>

</ru-elements>

</processing-elements>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

4.

TED NETCONF Server repeats Step-2.

5.

The O-DU NETCONF Client creates low-level-tx/rx-endpoints, e-axc-ids, tx/rx-array-carriers, low- level-

tx/rx-links. The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers <rpc><edit-config> with configuration data aligned

with TED module capabilities and user-plane-configuration data.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="7">

<edit-config>

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<default-operation>merge</default-operation>

<config>

<user-plane-configuration xmlns="urn:o-ran:uplane- conf:1.0">

<low-level-tx-endpoints>

<name>low-level-tx-endpoint-0</name>

<compression>

<compression-type>STATIC</compression-type>

<iq-bitwidth>14</iq-bitwidth>

<exponent>15</exponent>

</compression>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

123

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<frame-structure>193</frame-structure>

<cp-type>NORMAL</cp-type>

<cp-length>88</cp-length>

<cp-length-other>72</cp-length-other>

<offset-to-absolute-frequency-center>-3276</offset-to-

absolute-frequency-center>

<number-of-prb-per-scs>

<scs>KHZ_30</scs>

<number-of-prb>273</number-of-prb>

</number-of-prb-per-scs>

<e-axcid>

<o-du-port-bitmask>49152</o-du-port-bitmask>

<band-sector-bitmask>16128</band-sector-bitmask>

<ccid-bitmask>240</ccid-bitmask>

<ru-port-bitmask>15</ru-port-bitmask>

<eaxc-id>0</eaxc-id>

</e-axcid>

</low-level-tx-endpoints>

<low-level-rx-endpoints>

<name>low-level-rx-endpoint-0 </name>

<compression>

<compression-type>STATIC</compression-type>

<iq-bitwidth>14</iq-bitwidth>

<exponent>15</exponent>

</compression>

<frame-structure>193</frame-structure>

<cp-type>NORMAL</cp-type>

<cp-length>88</cp-length>

<cp-length-other>72</cp-length-other>

<offset-to-absolute-frequency-center>-3276</offset-to-

absolute-frequency-center>

<number-of-prb-per-scs>

<scs>KHZ_30</scs>

<number-of-prb>273</number-of-prb>

</number-of-prb-per-scs>

<ul-fft-sampling-offsets>

<scs>KHZ_30</scs>

<ul-fft-sampling-offset>21</ul-fft-sampling-offset>

</ul-fft-sampling-offsets>

<e-axcid>

<o-du-port-bitmask>49152</o-du-port-bitmask>

<band-sector-bitmask>16128</band-sector-bitmask>

<ccid-bitmask>240</ccid-bitmask>

<ru-port-bitmask>15</ru-port-bitmask>

<eaxc-id>0</eaxc-id>

</e-axcid>

<non-time-managed-delay-enabled>false</non-time-managed-

delay-enabled>

</low-level-rx-endpoints>

<tx-array-carriers>

<name>TxArrayCarrier0</name>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

124

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<absolute-frequency-center>643334</absolute-frequency-center>

<center-of-channel-bandwidth>3550000000</center-of-channel-

bandwidth>

<channel-bandwidth>100000000</channel-bandwidth>

<gain>28.5</gain>

<downlink-radio-frame-offset>2394880</downlink-radio-frame-

offset>

<downlink-sfn-offset>360</downlink-sfn-offset>

</tx-array-carriers>

<rx-array-carriers>

<name>RxArrayCarrier0</name>

<absolute-frequency-center>643334</absolute-frequency-center>

<center-of-channel-bandwidth>3550000000</center-of-channel-

bandwidth>

<channel-bandwidth>100000000</channel-bandwidth>

<downlink-radio-frame-offset>2394880</downlink-radio-frame-

offset>

<downlink-sfn-offset>360</downlink-sfn-offset>

<gain-correction>0.0</gain-correction>

<n-ta-offset>25600</n-ta-offset>

</rx-array-carriers>

<low-level-tx-links>

<name>LowLevelTxLink0</name>

<processing-element>processing-element01</processing-

element>

<tx-array-carrier>TxArrayCarrier0</tx-array-carrier>

<low-level-tx-endpoint>Low-Level-Tx-Endpoint-0</low-level-tx-

endpoint>

</low-level-tx-links>

<low-level-rx-links>

<name>LowLevelRxLink0</name>

<processing-element>processing-element01</processing-element>

<rx-array-carrier>RxArrayCarrier0</rx-array-carrier>

<low-level-rx-endpoint>Low-Level-Rx-Endpoint-0</low-level-rx-

endpoint>

</low-level-rx-links>

</user-plane-configuration>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

6.

If the <edit-config> data aligns with the TED NETCONF Server module-capabilities and uplane-

configuration, the TED responds with a <rpc-reply> ok message indicating successful completion of

requested procedure. Netconf config change notification is generated from TED NETCONF Server similar

to Step-2.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="8">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

125

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test steps 1-6 are performed successfully.

3.1a.10 ALD Communications

3.1a.10.1

ALD Communications Test

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This test is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU, if the O-DU supports ALD control.

The purpose of the test is to validate proper <ald-communication> RPCs exchange between O-DU and TED

NETCONF Server.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



14.4 Operational aspects of Antenna Line Devices

B.

Test Entrance Criteria



The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are

operational, configured and connected to the O-DU.



The TED is compliant with the following specifications:

1.

ISO/IEC 13239:2002 “High-level data link control (HDLC) procedures”

2.

Antenna Interface Standards Group AISG 2.0 (2006): "Control interface for antenna line devices"

3.

3GPP TS 37.462 (V15.2.0): "Iuant interface: Signalling transport"



ALD parameters like device type, vendor-code, etc. can be configured in the TED.



The <ald-communication> RPCs and flow might vary according to the O-DU implementation.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clause 3.1a.4.1 is successfully completed.

2.

A manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED) to initiate ALD procedure.

b.

Procedure

1)

O-DU NETCONF Client triggers DC voltage on desired ALD ports using <edit-config> rpc

to TED NETCONF Server.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="4">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<default-operation>merge</default-operation>

<config>

<ald-ports-io xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald-port:1.0">

<ald-port-dc-control>

<name>port1</name>

<dc-enabled>true</dc-enabled>

</ald-port-dc-control>

</ald-ports-io>

</config>

</edit-config>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

126

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

</rpc>

2)

TED NETCONF Server responds with rpc-reply

<rpc-reply><ok/></rpc-reply>

3)

After DC is turned on, O-DU waits for at least 3s

4)

O-DU NETCONF Client sends regular <ald-communication> RPC messages with “ald-req-msg” record

to TED NETCONF Server.

5)

O-DU NETCONF Client triggers <ald-communication> rpc for Device Scan to TED NETCONF Server.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="6">

<ald-communication xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id>1</port-id>

<ald-req-msg>FFBF81F01001035811150303FF17FF</ald-req-msg>

</ald-communication>

</rpc>

6)

TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply><output><status> as ACCEPTED and a <ald-resp-

msg>. The ald-resp-msg contains complete unique-id, vendor code and device type of the scanned ALD.

(see TS 37.462 for more information)

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="7">

<output xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">1</port-id>

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">ACCEPTED</status>

<ald-resp-msg xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0"> 00BF

81F00F01065859075BCD1506025859040101 </ald-resp-msg>

</output>

</rpc-reply>

7)

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers the Address Assignment ALD request RPC to TED NETCONF

Server to assign HDLC address to Secondary Device (ALD) (see TS 37.462 for more information)

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="8">

<ald-communication xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id>1</port-id>

<ald-req-msg> FF BF 81 F0 0F 01 06 58 59 07 5b CD 15 06 02 58 59 02 01 17 </ald-req-

msg>

</ald-communication>

</rpc>

8)

The TED NETCONF Server responds with Secondary Device information if available and matched with

the unique id, vendor code and device type if provided in the ald-req-msg.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="9">

<output xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">1</port-id>

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">ACCEPTED</status>

<ald-resp-msg xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0"> 17 BF 81 F0 0B 01 06 58 59 07 5b CD

15 04 01 01 </ald-resp-msg>

</output>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

127

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

</rpc-reply>

(Here, HDLC Address 17 has been assigned to ALD with Unique ID: 5859075BCD15 and Device type :

01 which is single-antenna RET)

9)

The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers the SNRM RPC to TED NETCONF Server for ALD Link

establishment. (See ISO/IEC 13239 for more information)

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="10">

<ald-communication xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id>1</port-id>

<ald-req-msg> 17 93</ald-req-msg>

</ald-communication>

</rpc>

The TED NETCONF Server responds with UA message with status ACCEPTED.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="11">

<output xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">1</port-id>

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">ACCEPTED</status>

<ald-resp-msg xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0"> 17 CE 73</ald-resp-msg>

</output>

</rpc-reply>

10) The O-DU NETCONF Client sends a get information elementary procedure message to get details of the

RET device with respective procedure-id. The O-DU NETCONF Client triggers the I-frame RPC to TED

NETCONF Server.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="12">

<ald-communication xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id>1</port-id>

<ald-req-msg> 17 10 05 00 </ald-req-msg>

</ald-communication>

</rpc>

11) The TED NETCONF Server responds with Secondary Device/ALD information with status ACCEPTED

and ald-resp-msg.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="13">

<output xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">1</port-id>

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">ACCEPTED</status>

<ald-resp-msg xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0"> 17 10 05 01 00 04 58 59 30 31 04 41

42 30 31 00 00 </ald-resp-msg>

</output>

</rpc-reply>

NOTE : common get-information elementary procedure referred from ETSI TS 125 463.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

128

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

12) O-DU NETCONF Client disconnects the HDLC link established for secondary devices by sending DISC

command. (See ISO/IEC 13239 for more information)

NOTE: There is no time constraint for DU to disconnect the secondary device.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="14">

<ald-communication xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id>0</port-id>

<ald-req-msg>17 53</ald-req-msg>

</ald-communication>

</rpc>

13) TED NETCONF Server responds with UA message with status ACCEPTED.

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="15">

<ald-communication xmlns="urn:o-ran:ald:1.0">

<port-id>0</port-id>

<status> ACCEPTED </status>

<ald-resp-msg>17 73</ald-resp-msg>

</ald-communication>

</rpc-reply>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Proper <ald-communication> rpcs are exchanged between O-DU NETCONF Client and TED NETCONF Server.

3.1a.11 Log Management

3.1a.11.1

Troubleshooting Test

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU that supports troubleshooting log management.

The test is applicable for DU with appropriate access privileges, namely “sudo”, “nms” or “fm-pm”.

This test validates the capability of the O-DU to handle troubleshooting logs including alarm information.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



14.2.2 Troubleshooting Log Management

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

129

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clauses 3.1a.2.1 and 3.1a.4.1 is successfully completed.

2.

A manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED) to initiate troubleshooting

log procedure

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <rpc><start-troubleshooting-logs> to the TED NETCONF Server.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply>< troubleshooting-status-grouping >.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="56">

<start-troubleshooting-logs xmlns="urn:o-

ran:troubleshooting:1.0"/>

</rpc>

<rpc-reply message-id="56"

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:troubleshooting:1.0">SUCCESS</status>

</rpc-reply>

2.

The TED NETCONF Server starts generating one or more file(s) containing troubleshooting logs. When

the generation of the log file(s) are completed, the TED NETCONF Server sends

<notification><troubleshooting-log-generated> with a list of one or more log file names to the O-DU

NETCONF Client.

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<troubleshooting-log-generated xmlns="urn:o-

ran:troubleshooting:1.0">

<log-file-name>o-ran/log/Troubleshootinglog.gz</log-file-name>

</troubleshooting-log-generated>

</notification>

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <rpc><file-upload>log-file-name</file-upload></rpc> to the TED

NETCONF Server to start uploading the log file(s). The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-

reply><file-upload> with status SUCCESS.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

130

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="57">

<file-upload xmlns="urn:o-ran:file-management:1.0">

<local-logical-file-path>o-ran/log/Troubleshootinglog.gz</local-

logical-file-path>

<remote-file-path>sftp://oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/local/log</remote-

file-path>

<password>

<password>1234</password>

</password>

</file-upload>

</rpc>

<rpc-reply message-id="57"

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:file-management:1.0">SUCCESS</status>

</rpc-reply>

4.

The TED NETCONF Server uploads the log file(s) to NETCONF Client by sFTP/FTPES, using FTPES if

the NETCONF Server controller is using NETCONF/TLS to configure the TED. When file upload is

completed, the TED NETCONF Server sends <notification><upload-notification> with status SUCCESS.

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<file-upload-notification xmlns="urn:o-ran:file-management:1.0">

<local-logical-file-path>o-ran/log/Troubleshootinglog.gz</local-

logical-file-path>

<remote-file-path>sftp://oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/local/log

</remote-file-path>

<status>SUCCESS</status>

</file-upload-notification>

</notification>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test steps 1-4 are performed successfully.

3.1a.11.2

Trace Test

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU that supports trace log management.

The test is applicable for DU with appropriate access privileges, namely “sudo”, “nms” or “fm-pm”.

This test validates the capability of the O-DU to handle trace logs.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



14.3 Trace

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

131

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clauses 3.1a.2.1 and 3.1a.4.1 is successfully completed.

2.

A manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED) to initiate trace log

procedure

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <rpc><start-trace-logs> to the TED NETCONF Server. The TED

NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply> <start-trace-logs>.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="57">

<start-trace-logs xmlns="urn:o-ran:trace:1.0"/>

</rpc>

<rpc-reply message-id=”57”

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:trace:1.0">SUCCESS</status>

</rpc-reply>

2.

The TED NETCONF Server starts generating one or more file(s) containing trace logs. When the

generation of the log file(s) are completed, the TED NETCONF Server sends <notification><trace-log-

generated> and <is-notification-last> set as false with a list of one or more log file names to the O-DU

NETCONF Client.

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<trace-log-generated xmlns="urn:o-ran:trace:1.0">

<log-file-name>o-ran/log/Tracelog.gz</log-file-name>

<is-notification-last>false</is-notification-last>

</trace-log-generated>

</notification>

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <rpc><file-upload>log-file-name</file-upload></rpc> to the TED

NETCONF Server to start uploading the log file(s). The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-

reply><file-upload> with status SUCCESS.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="58">

<file-upload xmlns="urn:o-ran:file-management:1.0">

<local-logical-file-path>o-ran/log/TraceLog.gz</local-logical-

file-path>

<remote-file-path>sftp://

oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/local/log</remote-file-path>

<password>

<password>1234</password>

</password>

</file-upload>

</rpc>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

132

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<rpc-reply message-id=”58”

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:file management:1.0">SUCCESS</status>

</rpc-reply>

4.

The TED NETCONF Server uploads the log file(s) to NETCONF Client by sFTP/FTPES, using FTPES if

the NETCONF Server controller is using NETCONF/TLS to configure the TED. When file upload is

completed, the TED NETCONF Server sends <notification><upload-notification> with status SUCCESS.

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<file-upload-notification xmlns="urn:o-ran:file-management:1.0">

<local-logical-file-path>o-ran/log/Tracelog.gz</local-logical-

file-path>

<remote-file-path>sftp://oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/local/

log</remote-file-path>

<status>SUCCESS</status>

</file-upload-notification>

</notification>

5.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <rpc><stop-trace-logs> to the TED NETCONF Server. The TED

NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply> <stop-trace-logs>.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="59">

<stop-trace-logs xmlns="urn:o-ran:trace:1.0"/>

</rpc>

<rpc-reply message-id="59"

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:trace:1.0">SUCCESS</status>

</rpc-reply>

6.

The O-DU NETCONF Client can wait for the notification from the TED NETCONF Server. The TED

NETCONF Server sends <notification> and set <is-notification-last> to true.

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime> YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ </eventTime>

<trace-log-generated xmlns="urn:o-ran:trace:1.0">

<log-file-name>o-ran/log/Tracelog.gz</log-file-name>

<is-notification-last>true</is-notification-last>

</trace-log-generated>

</notification>

7.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <rpc><file-upload>log-file-name</file-upload></rpc> to the TED

NETCONF Server to start uploading the remaining log file(s). The TED NETCONF Server responds with

<rpc-reply><file-upload> with status SUCCESS.

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="58">

<file-upload xmlns="urn:o-ran:file-management:1.0">

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

133

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<local-logical-file-path>o-ran/log/TraceLog.gz</local-logical-

file-path>

<remote-file-path>sftp://

oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/local/log</remote-file-path>

<password>

<password>1234</password>

</password>

</file-upload>

</rpc>

<rpc-reply message-id=”58”

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<status xmlns="urn:o-ran:file management:1.0">SUCCESS</status>

</rpc-reply>

8.

The TED NETCONF Server uploads the remaining log file(s) to NETCONF Client by sFTP/FTPES,

using FTPES if the Netonf Server controller is using NETCONF/TLS to configure the TED. When file

upload is completed, the TED NETCONF Server sends <notification><upload-notification> with status

SUCCESS.

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<file-upload-notification xmlns="urn:o-ran:file-management:1.0">

<local-logical-file-path>o-ran/log/Tracelog.gz</local-logical-

file-path>

<remote-file-path>sftp://oranuser@192.168.3.13:22/local/

log</remote-file-path>

<status>SUCCESS</status>

</file-upload-notification>

</notification>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test step 1-8 are performed successfully.

3.1a.12 Connectivity Check

3.1a.12.1

Ethernet Connectivity Monitoring

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY if the O-DU supports C-Plane and U-Plane connectivity over

Ethernet.

This test validates the proper operation of LBM.

This scenario corresponds to the following clauses in [3]:



7.3 C/U Plane VLAN configuration



7.5 Definition of processing elements



7.6.1 C/U-Plane transport connectivity verification

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

134

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

1.

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are

operational, configured and connected to the O-DU.

2.

Result validation of this test requires TED capabilities such as a network sniffer that allows for traffic analysis

over ETH frames between O-DU and TED.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clauses 3.1a.4.1 and 3.1a.9.1 are successfully completed.

2.

Based on the O-DU implementation, Ethernet Connectivity Monitoring may be tested either during the

“start-up” procedure or alternatively initiated via a manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a

command from the TED)

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client configures a transport-flow in the processing element: a combination of

VLAN identity, O-DU MAC address and O-RU MAC address.

2.

The O-DU sends loopback message to the O-RU (TED) MAC address configured for the processing

element.

3.

The TED sends loopback responses to the O-DU MAC Address of the loopback message.

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Packets are sent correctly in step 2 and 3.

3.1a.12.2

UDP Echo Test

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY if the O-DU supports C-Plane and U-Plane sessions over IP.

This test validates the proper operation UDP Echo for continuous verification of connectivity.

This scenario corresponds to the following clauses in [3]:



7.4 O-RU C/U Plane IP Address Assignment



7.5 Definition of processing elements



7.6 O-DU Verification of C/U Plane Transport Connectivity

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

1.

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are

operational, configured and connected to the O-DU.

2.

Result validation of this test requires TED capabilities such as a network sniffer that allows for traffic analysis

over ETH frames between O-DU and TED.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clauses 3.1a.4.1 and 3.1a.9.1 are successfully completed.

2.

Based on the O-DU implementation, UDP Echo Test may be tested during the “start-up” procedure or

alternatively initiated via a manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED).

b.

Procedure

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

135

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client configures IP information and udpip-flow in the processing element for the

C-Plane and U-Plane communications.

2.

The O-DU NETCONF Client configures the enable-udp-echo leaf = true in the udp-echo YANG model to

be enabled operation of the UDP echo server in the TED.

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends a UDP datagram to UDP port number:7 of the TED NETCONF

Server.

4.

The TED NETCONF Server sends back the UDP datagram to the O-DU NETCONF Client.

5.

The O-DU NETCONF Client configures the enable-udp-echo leaf = false in the udp-echo YANG model

to be disabled operation of the UDP echo server in the TED

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Packets are sent correctly in step 3 and 4.

3.1a.13 Energy Savings

3.1a.13.1

M-Plane based TRX control

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU. The test is executed if the O-DU supports M-Plane

based TRX-control.

This test validates M-plane based TRX control.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:



20.3.2 TRX control – M-Plane controlled



10.2 Measurement activation and de-activation

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU

 

C.

Test Methodology

a. Initial Conditions .

1.

The TED NETCONF Server exposes the capability to support energy saving

2.

The TED NETCONF Server exposes the capability to support MPLANE-TRX-CONTROL

3.

The antenna mask is set to enable all array-elements.

4.

A manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED) to initiate M-Plane based

TRX Control.

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF client sets the value of antenna mask for the [tr]x-array. It is recommended to set at

least half of the antenna mask off in order to have more differentiable power changes.

2.

For the case where the TED NETCONF Server advertises mplane-supported-trx-control-masks list, RPC

edit-config shall be used to configure a specific antenna mask value by specifying a given antenna-

bitmask-index for the [tr]x-array using the YANG parameter [tr]x-array-antenna-mask-config.

EXAMPLE-1: configuration of a given 32-bit tx antenna mask by specifying the antenna-index 1, which

points to an antenna mask that has half ones and the other half zeros:

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

136

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="101">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

<array-name> tx-array-1 </array-name>

<antenna-bitmask-index> 1 </antenna-bitmask-index>

</tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

For the case where the TED NETCONF Server does not advertise mplane-supported-trx-control-

masks list for a given [tr]x-array, the RPC edit-config shall be used to configure a specific antenna-

bitmask value for the [tr]x-array using the YANG parameter [tr]x-array-antenna-mask-config.

EXAMPLE-2: configuration of a 32-bit tx antenna mask is shown below:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="101">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

<array-name>tx-array-1</array-name>

<antenna-bitmask>11111111000000000000000011111111</antenna-

bitmask >

</tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

3.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> message indicating successful completion of

requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="101">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

137

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

4.

The O-DU NETCONF Client waits for mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update notification. 

EXAMPLE-1: mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update notification by specifying the antenna-index is

shown below:

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

<array-name>tx-array-1</array-name>

<array-type>tx-array</array-type>

<updated-antenna-mask-index>1</updated-antenna-mask-index>

</mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

EXAMPLE-2: mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update notification by specifying antenna mask is shown

below:

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

<array-name>tx-array-1</array-name>

<array-type>tx-array</array-type>

<updated-antenna-mask>11111111000000000000000011111111</updated-

antenna-mask>

</mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

</notification>

5.

The O-DU NETCONF client resets the value of antenna mask for the [tr]x-array.

For the case where the TED NETCONF Server advertises mplane-supported-trx-control-masks list, RPC

edit-config shall be used to configure a specific antenna mask value by specifying a given antenna-

bitmask-index for the [tr]x-array using the YANG parameter [tr]x-array-antenna-mask-config.

EXAMPLE-1: configuration of a given 32-bit tx antenna mask by specifying the antenna-index 5 which

points to all-ones antenna mask:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="102">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

<array-name>tx-array-1</array-name>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

138

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<antenna-bitmask-index>5</antenna-bitmask-index>

</tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

For the case where the TED NETCONF server does not advertise mplane-supported-trx-control-

masks list for a given [tr]x-array, the RPC edit-config shall be used to configure a specific antenna-

bitmask value for the [tr]x-array using the YANG parameter [tr]x-array-antenna-mask-config.

EXAMPLE-2: configuration of a 32-bit tx antenna mask is shown below:

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="102">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

<array-name>tx-array-1</array-name>

<antenna-bitmask>11111111111111111111111111111111</antenna-bitmask >

</tx-array-antenna-mask-config>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

6.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> message indicating successful completion

of requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0"

message-id="102">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

7.

The O-DU NETCONF client waits for mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update notification. 

For Example,

EXAMPLE 1: mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update notification by specifying the antenna-index is shown

below:

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

139

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

<array-name>tx-array-1</array-name>

<array-type>tx-array</array-type>

<updated-antenna-mask-index>5</updated-antenna-mask-

index>

</mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

EXAMPLE-2: mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update notification by specifying antenna mask is shown

below:

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

<array-name>tx-array-1</array-name>

<array-type>tx-array</array-type>

<updated-antenna-mask>11111111111111111111111111111111</updated-

antenna-mask>

</mplane-trx-control-ant-mask-update>

</notification>

 

B.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test steps above are performed successfully.

3.1a.13.2

Deep-hibernate

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU. The test is executed if the TED/O-RU O-DU supports

M-Plane based deep-hibernate control.

This test validates deep-hibernation.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:

20.5 Deep-hibernate

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU

C.

Test Methodology

a. Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clause 3.1a.9.1 is successfully completed .

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

140

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

2.

The TED NETCONF Server exposes the capability to support energy savings including DEEP-

HIBERNATE

3.

A manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED) to initiate deep-hibernation

procedure.

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends <get> rpc for module-capabilities.

2.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with <rpc-reply> with module-capabilties data including min-

hibernate-time-duration and max-hibernate-time-duration

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends deep-hibernate rpc command with a hibernate-time withing the range

of TED module-capabilities.

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="105">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<deep-hibernate>

<hibernate-time>15</hibernate-time>

</deep-hibernate>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

4.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> message indicating successful completion of

requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="105">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

5.

The O-DU NETCONF Client waits for deep-hibernate-activated notification.

For Example,

<notification

xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<deep-hibernate-activated/>

<hibernate-time>15</hibernate-time>

<deep-hibernate-activated/>

</notification>

6.

The TED NETCONF Server starts deep-hibernate timer.

7.

The O-DU NETCONF Client sends close-session rpc command.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

141

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="106">

<close-session xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0"/>

</rpc>

8.

The TED NETCONF Server restarts after the expiry of hibernate-time.

9.

After restart, the O-DU Netconf Client verifies restart-cause value is DEEP-HIBERNATE-RESTART.

 

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test steps above are performed successfully.

3.1a.13.3

Data layer control

A.

Test Description and Applicability

This scenario is CONDITIONAL MANDATORY for O-DU. The test is executed if the O-DU supports M-Plane

based Data layer control.

This test validates Data layer control.

This scenario corresponds to the following clause in [3]:

20.3.3 Data layer control

10.2 Measurement activation and de-activation

B.

Test Entrance Criteria

The minimum functions of the TED described in clause 2.2 that support validation of the M-Plane are operational,

configured and connected to the O-DU.

C.

Test Methodology

a.

Initial Conditions

1.

The test procedure described in clause 3.1a.9.1 is successfully completed

2.

The TED NETCONF Server exposes the capability to support energy savings including MPLANE-

DATA-LAYER-CONTROL

3.

A manual/automation trigger (e.g., DUT receives a command from the TED) to initiate data layer control

procedure.

b.

Procedure

1.

The O-DU NETCONF Client turns off data layers by adding energy-sharing-group-id into energy-sharing-

groups-disabled leaf-list parameter.

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="103">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<energy-sharing-groups-disabled>4</energy-sharing-groups-

disabled>

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

142

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

2.

The TED NETCONF Server updates energy-sharing-groups-disabled and responds with a <rpc-reply>

message indicating successful completion of requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="103">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

3.

The O-DU NETCONF Client waits for 10 seconds.

4.

The O-DU NETCONF Client turns on data layers by removing energy-sharing-group-id from energy-

sharing-groups-disabled leaf-list parameter

For Example,

<rpc xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="104">

<edit-config xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0">

<target>

<running/>

</target>

<config>

<energy-sharing-groups-disabled:operation="delete">4</energy-

sharing-groups-disabled>

</config>

</edit-config>

</rpc>

5.

The TED NETCONF Server responds with a <rpc-reply> message indicating successful completion of

requested procedure.

For Example,

<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-

id="104">

<ok/>

</rpc-reply>

6.

The O-DU NETCONF Client waits for data-layer-control-wakeup-notification.

Example,

<notification xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:notification:1.0">

<eventTime>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.FZ</eventTime>

<data-layer-control-wakeup-notification>

<energy-sharing-group>4</energy-sharing-group>

</data-layer-control-wakeup-notification>

</notification>

D.

Test Requirement (expected result)

Test steps above are performed successfully.

________________________________________________________________________________________________

© 2025 by the O-RAN ALLIANCE e.V. Your use is subject to the copyright statement on the cover page of this specification.

143

O-RAN.WG4.TS.CONF.0-R005-v15.00

3.2

UC-Plane Measurements O-RU

3.2.1

UC-Plane Standard Test Definitions

The following clauses describe test scenarios for equipment implementing the U-Plane and C-Plane O-RAN option 7.2x

protocols.

3.2.1.1

UC-Plane Measurements, O-RU TEST SCENARIO

In these sets of tests, the Device Under Test (DUT) is the O-RU.

This document describes conformance tests for 4 scenarios:

1.

Conducted FDD tests for FR1 radios

2.

Non-conducted (OTA) FDD tests for FR1 radios

3.

Conducted TDD tests for FR1 radios

4.

Non-conducted tests for TDD FR1 and FR2 radios

The O-RU must only be tested for the scenarios that apply to the O-RU capabilities. The fact that there are 4 scenarios

defined does not imply that every O-RU must be tested for all scenarios in order to be O-RAN compliant and

consequently the O-RU does not have to support all the capabilities required to test all 4 scenarios (i.e. support of FR1,

FR2, TDD, FDD, conducted and non-conducted). In other words, depending on whether the O-RU supports capabilities

such as FDD, TDD, FR1 and or FR2, only the corresponding scenarios that match the O-RU capabilities will apply. For

example, an O-RU that does not have or cannot provide access to antenna ports (or TAB connectors), supports TDD

and operates at FR2 bands, the corresponding scenario that the O-RU will need to be tested to be O-RAN conformant is

scenario #4 only. Another example, for an O-RU that supports FDD, operates at FR1 bands and has or can provide

access to antenna ports (or TAB connectors), scenarios #1 or #2 can apply but only one of the two possible scenarios

will be selected to test the O-RU for O-RAN conformance.

For scenario #1 and #3 above the RF test setup is as described in ETSI TS 138.141-1[NR] and TS 136.141 [LTE]. For

scenarios #2 and #4 the RF test setup is as described in TS 138.141-2. Note that O-RAN conformance testing is
